text.title: direnv + mise: Configuration
text.summary: Full .envrc for a Python project: `use mise` activates runtime, `layout python-venv .venv` creates/activates virtualenv, `dotenv_if_exists .env` loads secrets, `watch_file` triggers reload on dependency changes.

example.code:
# .envrc use mise # activate mise-managed Python version
layout python-venv .venv # create/activate .venv with mise Python
dotenv_if_exists .env # load secrets (never commit .env)
watch_file requirements.txt watch_file pyproject.toml
PATH_add scripts
export PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 export PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

section.title: mise.toml (Native Format)
section.p: Prefer mise.toml for new projects — supports tasks, env vars, and configuration beyond what .tool-versions allows.

example.code:
# mise.toml [tools] python = "3.12.8" node = "22.12.0"
[env] PROJECT_NAME = "my-project"
[tasks.lint] run = "ruff check ."
[tasks.test] run = "pytest"

list.item: `layout python-venv .venv` — create .venv with current Python, activate
